 {"id":"brenner_bot-28j","title":"Bug: Avoid duplicate window storage listeners during HMR","description":"Problem: apps/web/src/stores/readingStore.ts attaches a window "storage" listener with an inline handler and never removes it. In dev/HMR, this can lead to duplicate listeners and repeated updates; UBS also flags it.

Fix: Use a stable handler reference and remove any prior handler during module re-init (store handler on globalThis under a unique key), then add the listener once.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"PurplePond","created_at":"2025-12-30T08:41:43.595740468-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-30T08:49:59.096077737-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-30T08:49:59.096077737-05:00","close_reason":"Prevent duplicate readingStore subscriptions/storage listeners during HMR","labels":["reliability","web"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"brenner_bot-28j","depends_on_id":"brenner_bot-5so.4","type":"discovered-from","created_at":"2025-12-30T08:41:43.600166803-05:00","created_by":"daemon","metadata":"{}"}]}
 {"id":"brenner_bot-2em","title":"Build distillations comparison view","description":"# Task

Create the split-pane comparison view for viewing distillations side-by-side.

# Scope (Reduced)

This task focuses ONLY on the split-pane synchronized view. Other components are now separate tasks:
- Overview page: brenner_bot-0li
- Crosswalk table: brenner_bot-hog

# Split View Features

1. **2 or 3 pane layout** - User can choose how many models to compare
2. **Model selector** - Dropdown to choose which model in each pane
3. **Synchronized scrolling** - Scroll in one pane, others follow
4. **Section alignment** - Attempt to align similar sections

# Implementation

```tsx
<SplitComparison>
  <Pane model='opus' />
  <Pane model='gpt' />
  <Pane model='gemini' />
</SplitComparison>
```

# Sync Scroll Logic

- Use IntersectionObserver or scroll event
- Calculate relative scroll position
- Apply to other panes with same ratio
- Option to disable sync

# Responsive

- Desktop: 2-3 panes side by side
- Mobile: Tabbed view (switch between models)

# Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Split view renders 2-3 panes
- [ ] Model selection works
- [ ] Sync scroll functions correctly
- [ ] Responsive mobile fallback","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-29T19:15:02.358741387-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-29T21:37:04.89367459-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-29T21:37:04.89367459-05:00","close_reason":"Implemented: Split-pane compare view with sync scroll, model selectors, mobile tabs at /distillations/compare","labels":["compare","distillations","ux","web"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"brenner_bot-2em","depends_on_id":"brenner_bot-0li","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-29T19:21:00.323253462-05:00","created_by":"daemon","metadata":"{}"},{"issue_id":"brenner_bot-2em","depends_on_id":"brenner_bot-igw","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-12-29T19:23:47.723522564-05:00","created_by":"daemon","metadata":"{}"}]}
 {"id":"brenner_bot-2ho","title":"Bug: AgentMailClient should parse SSE (text/event-stream)","description":"Agent Mail contract allows SSE responses (Content-Type: text/event-stream).

Problem: AgentMailClient currently assumes JSON and JSON.parse()s the body, which fails on SSE streams.

Fix: In apps/web/src/lib/agentMail.ts, detect text/event-stream and parse SSE frames (data: lines). Return the last JSON-RPC payload result (or error) and keep behavior identical for application/json.

Add a unit test that stubs fetch to return an SSE payload with one or more JSON-RPC result events.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"PurplePond","created_at":"2025-12-30T08:41:38.440876032-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-30T08:49:53.969462671-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-30T08:49:53.969462671-05:00","close_reason":"Implemented SSE parsing for AgentMailClient.call() + added unit tests","labels":["agent-mail","cli","reliability","web"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"brenner_bot-2ho","depends_on_id":"brenner_bot-5so.3.1","type":"discovered-from","created_at":"2025-12-30T08:41:38.466791215-05:00","created_by":"daemon","metadata":"{}"}]}
-{"id":"brenner_bot-2iwb","title":"TASK: Assumption Ladder - Link assumptions to Test Queue","description":"# Assumption Ladder → Test Queue Linking

## Goal
Make each assumption independently testable by linking it to discriminative tests in the session Test Queue.

## Scope
- Add a way to attach one or more `assumptionId` references to a test.
- Add UI affordance from the Assumption Ladder to create/link a test.
- Show linked assumptions in the Test Queue UI.

## Notes
- Keep this local/UI-first (no vendor AI APIs).
- Prefer minimal schema changes and reuse existing storage patterns.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Tests can reference assumptions (one-to-many)
- [ ] Assumption Ladder can create/link a test
- [ ] Test Queue displays linked assumptions
- [ ] Storage persists links across reloads

 {"id":"brenner_bot-2n1","title":"Optional: install and configure Framer Motion","description":"# Task

Set up Framer Motion for animations throughout the app.

# Installation

```bash
cd apps/web && bun add framer-motion
```

# Configuration

Create motion wrapper for Next.js compatibility:
```tsx
// components/motion.tsx
'use client';
export { motion, AnimatePresence } from 'framer-motion';
export const springs = {
  smooth: { type: 'spring', stiffness: 300, damping: 30 },
  snappy: { type: 'spring', stiffness: 500, damping: 35 },
};
export const fadeUp = {
  initial: { opacity: 0, y: 20 },
  animate: { opacity: 1, y: 0 },
};
```

# Animation Presets

- fadeUp: Content entering from below
- fadeScale: Content scaling up from 95%
- staggerContainer: Parent for staggered children

# Reduced Motion

Always check `prefers-reduced-motion`:
```tsx
const prefersReducedMotion = useReducedMotion();
if (prefersReducedMotion) return staticContent;
```

# Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Framer Motion installed
- [ ] Motion wrapper created
- [ ] Animation presets defined
- [ ] Reduced motion hook available
- [ ] Sample animation works","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-29T19:16:39.79123191-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-30T13:19:36.677802271-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-30T13:19:36.677802271-05:00","close_reason":"Framer Motion already installed (v12.23.26) and in use (SessionActions.tsx, OperatorsClient.tsx)","labels":["animation","framer-motion","ux","web"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"brenner_bot-2n1","depends_on_id":"brenner_bot-7yz","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-29T19:16:48.144124896-05:00","created_by":"daemon","metadata":"{}"},{"issue_id":"brenner_bot-2n1","depends_on_id":"brenner_bot-1qx","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-12-29T19:23:27.97186143-05:00","created_by":"daemon","metadata":"{}"}]}
 {"id":"brenner_bot-2qyl","title":"Feature: Research Program Dashboard","description":"# Feature: Research Program Dashboard

## Purpose
Aggregate research progress across multiple sessions into a coherent program view. Enable researchers to understand the overall state of their research without reading every session artifact.

## Background
The Brenner method naturally spans multiple sessions. Currently, there is no way to get a high-level view. This dashboard provides that view.

## Research Program Concept

A **Research Program** groups related sessions:

```typescript
interface ResearchProgram {
  id: string;
  name: string;
  description: string;
  sessions: string[];
  createdAt: string;
  status: "active" | "paused" | "completed" | "abandoned";
  notes?: string;
}
```

## Dashboard Components

### 1. Hypothesis Funnel
Visual representation of hypothesis lifecycle with breakdown by origin:
```
Proposed: 12 (8 original, 3 third-alternative, 1 anomaly-spawned)
    ↓
Active: 5  |  Under Attack: 2  |  Assumption-Undermined: 1
    ↓
Killed: 6  |  Validated: 0  |  Dormant: 1
```

### 2. Registry Health Metrics

**Hypothesis Registry:**
- Total hypotheses: Count by state
- Kill rate: Killed / (Killed + Active + Validated)
- Third alternative coverage: % of hypothesis sets with third alt

**Assumption Registry:**
- Unchecked assumptions: Count (warning if > 5)
- Scale/physics checks: Count (warning if 0!)
- Falsified assumptions: Count + impact (which hypotheses affected)

**Anomaly Registry:**
- Active anomalies: Count (unresolved puzzles)
- Deferred anomalies: Count (parked questions)
- Paradigm-shifting: Count (if > 0, major finding!)
- Resolution rate: Resolved / Total

**Critique Registry:**
- Active critiques: Count (unaddressed attacks)
- Critiques by severity: minor/moderate/serious/critical breakdown
- Response rate: Addressed / Total
- Hypothesis under attack: List (high unaddressed count)

### 3. Test Execution Metrics
```
Designed: 8  |  Executed: 3  |  Pending: 5
Potency checks: 3/8 (37%) ⚠️
Average evidence-per-week score: 7.2/12
```

### 4. Active Hypotheses
List of hypotheses in "active" state with threat level:
```
H-RS20251230-001: Cell fate is epigenetically determined [active, 2 critiques]
H-RS20251231-003: Third alternative: chromatin accessibility [active, clean]
```

### 5. Blocked Tests
Tests that are designed but not yet executed:
```
T-RS20251230-002: Lineage tracing with Cre-lox [designed, 3 days, missing potency!]
T-RS20251231-001: Single-cell ATAC-seq [designed, 1 day]
```

### 6. Unresolved Anomalies
Active anomalies that need attention:
```
X-RS20251230-001: AB lineage shows position-dependent fates [active, challenges H1, H2]
```

### 7. Assumption Warnings
Scale checks and falsified assumptions:
```
⚠️ No scale/physics assumptions recorded! Add at least one.
A-RS20251230-003: Diffusion timescale check [verified, τ ≈ 17 min]
A-RS20251231-001: Cell cycle length assumption [falsified → H2 undermined]
```

### 8. Key Findings
Auto-extracted from session artifacts:
- Killed hypotheses with reasoning
- Validated hypotheses (rare!)
- Falsified assumptions with cascade effects
- Resolved anomalies

### 9. Timeline
Chronological view of major events:
```
2025-12-30: Session RS-20251230 - 3 hypotheses proposed
2025-12-30: A1 (scale check) verified - gradient timing plausible
2025-12-31: H1 killed by T1 (unexpected methylation pattern)
2025-12-31: X1 spawned H4 (third alternative from anomaly)
2026-01-02: C3 (critical) addressed - H2 modified
```

## CLI Commands

- `brenner program create --name="..." --description="..."`
- `brenner program add-session <program-id> <session-id>`
- `brenner program dashboard <program-id>` — Full dashboard
- `brenner program dashboard <program-id> --section=hypotheses|assumptions|anomalies|critiques|tests`
- `brenner program health <program-id>` — Quick health check (warnings only)
- `brenner program export <program-id> --format=md|json`

## Health Check Warnings

The dashboard should surface warnings when:
- No scale/physics assumptions recorded
- Unchecked assumptions > 5
- Active critiques (especially severity=critical) > 3
- Active anomalies > 3
- Potency check coverage < 80%
- Any hypothesis has > 2 unaddressed critiques

## Success Criteria
- [ ] Research programs can be created and managed
- [ ] Sessions can be grouped into programs
- [ ] All four registries feed dashboard metrics
- [ ] Health check warnings implemented
- [ ] CLI generates readable dashboard output
- [ ] Timeline includes all registry events

## Depends On
- Hypothesis Registry Schema
- Hypothesis Lifecycle States
- Test→Hypothesis Binding
- Assumption Registry and Lifecycle
- Anomaly Registry
- Critique Registry
- Brenner Method Scorecard","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2025-12-30T19:41:47.171167739-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-31T03:10:45.226590456-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-31T03:10:45.226590456-05:00","close_reason":"All subtasks completed: schema, storage, CLI commands, metrics, and tests.  {"id":"brenner_bot-2vj","title":"Bug: DELTA subject mismatch breaks session status","description":"# Problem
`session start`/kickoff prompts instruct agents to respond with subjects like `DELTA[<role display name>]: ...` (e.g. `DELTA[Test Designer]: ...`).

`session status` derives role completion by parsing subjects and appears to only recognize `DELTA[<shorthand>]` where shorthand is one of: `gpt`, `codex`, `opus`, `claude`, `gemini` (etc). If agents follow kickoff instructions literally, deltas may not be counted and a thread can remain "incomplete" forever.

# Done when
- Kickoff instructions and status parsing agree on DELTA subject format.
- Status correctly counts deltas for each role in both CLI + web (tests added).

# Proposed fix
- Prefer canonical shorthand in kickoff instructions: `DELTA[gpt]`, `DELTA[opus]`, `DELTA[gemini]`.
- Make status parsing robust to role display names (accept a small synonym map) so older threads still work.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2025-12-30T01:41:17.466555154-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-30T01:53:29.365353032-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-30T01:53:29.365353032-05:00","close_reason":"Fixed DELTA subject mismatch: kickoff now uses shorthand tags; threadStatus parses both shorthand and role display names; added tests.","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"brenner_bot-2vj","depends_on_id":"brenner_bot-5so.5.3.2","type":"discovered-from","created_at":"2025-12-30T01:41:17.492550294-05:00","created_by":"daemon","metadata":"{}"}]}
